      "It is about the utility requirement for patent applications in the chemical field.",
      "It is about the degree of specific utility a claimed invention must have to qualify for patenting.",

      "It is about the interpretation of the \"useful\" requirement in patent law."

    "About": [
      "それは、特許法に関する米国最高裁判所の判決についてです。",
      "それは、化学分野における特許出願の実用性要件についてです。",
      "それは、特許を受けるために請求された発明が持つべき具体的な有用性の程度についてです。",
      "それは、特許庁の却下を審査するためのサーチナリティの利用可能性についてです。",
      "それは、特許可能な発明に対する「有用性」要件の解釈についてです。"
    ],

      "それは、最高裁判所による特許庁決定の審査プロセスに影響を与えます。",
      "それは、特許可能な発明に対する「有用性」要件の解釈に影響を与えます。"
    ],

      "それは、特許法と関連があります。",
      "それは、米国最高裁判所と関連があります。",

      "それは、「特許は狩猟免許ではない」という声明を持っています。",
      "それは、特許庁の却下を審査する際のサーチナリティの管轄権に関する決定を持っています。",
      "それは、有用性に関する実体特許法の問題に関する決定を持っています。",
      "それは、有用性に関する反対意見を持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは、特許出願における「実用性要件」によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは、発明に対する「具体的な有用性」基準によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは、「特許は狩猟免許ではない」という原則によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは、特許庁却下の審査に関する最高裁判所のサーチナリティ管轄権の確立によって特徴づけられます。",

      "それは、米国政府がCCPAの判決を審査する能力に影響を与えました。",
      "それは、特許法における「具体的な有用性」の理解に影響を与えました。"

About: It is about a decision of the United States Supreme Court concerning patent law.
It is about the utility requirement for patent applications in the chemical field.
It is about the degree of specific utility a claimed invention must have to qualify for patenting.
It is about the availability of certiorari jurisdiction for reviewing patent office rejections.
It is about the interpretation of the "useful" requirement in patent law.
Affects: It affects the requirements for obtaining a patent in the chemical field.
It affects the ability of patent applicants to claim monopolies on knowledge.
It affects the review process for patent office decisions by the Supreme Court.
It affects the interpretation of the "useful" requirement for patentable inventions.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with patent law.
It is associated with the United States Supreme Court.
It is associated with the utility requirement for patents.
It is associated with the concept of "specific utility".
BelongsTo: It belongs to United States Supreme Court case law.
HasA: It has a holding that a novel process for making a known steroid did not satisfy the utility requirement.
It has a ruling that a process patent in the chemical field requires specific utility.
It has a statement that a patent is not a hunting license.
It has a decision on the jurisdictional issue of reviewing patent office rejections.
It has a decision on the substantive patent law issue of utility.
It has a dissenting opinion on the utility issue.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the "utility requirement" for patent applications.
It is characterized by the "specific utility" standard for inventions.
It is characterized by the principle that a patent is not a hunting license.
It is characterized by the establishment of Supreme Court certiorari jurisdiction over patent office rejections.
It is characterized by a unanimous opinion on the jurisdictional issue.
It is characterized by a 7-2 opinion on the utility issue.
Influenced: It influenced subsequent patent law cases like In re Kirk and In re Joly.
It influenced the US Government's ability to seek review of CCPA judgments.
It influenced the understanding of "specific utility" in patent law.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the uncertainty regarding the Article III status of the CCPA.
It was influenced by the decision in Lurk v. United States.
IsA: It is a decision of the United States Supreme Court.
It is a landmark case in United States patent law.
It is a case that established the "specific utility" requirement for chemical patents.
It is a case that clarified the Supreme Court's certiorari jurisdiction over patent office decisions.
KnownAs: It is known as Brenner v. Manson.
KnownFor: It is known for the statement "a patent is not a hunting license."
It is known for holding that a novel process for making a known steroid did not satisfy the utility requirement.
It is known for the statement "a patent is not a hunting license."
It is known for establishing the "specific utility" standard for patentability in the chemical field.
It is known for clarifying Supreme Court's certiorari jurisdiction over patent office rejections.
It is known for the principle that a patent monopoly should only be granted if clearly commanded by statute.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United States.
PartOf: It is part of United States Supreme Court history.
It is part of the legal precedent regarding patent utility.
UsedIn: It is used in legal discussions about patent law.
It is used in discussions about the "utility requirement" for patents.
It is used in discussions about the "specific utility" standard.
It is used in discussions about the "hunting license" analogy for patents.
